name: shikanime-studio/skills
description: A strict development workflow for Hermes and compatible agents: spec-as-issue pipeline + always-on minimalism discipline, with feature-driven and property-based stability tests. The minimalism discipline is woven through the pipeline, not a separate skill.

skills.name: ask
skills.description: Router over the workflow catalog. Ask which skill or flow fits your situation and get pointed at the right one. Use when unsure where to start.

skills.name: code-review
skills.description: Three-axis review of a diff against a fixed point: Standards (does it follow this repo's coding standards?), Spec (does it match the originating issue/PRD?), and Stability (were the spec's property-based tests shipped?). Runs axes as parallel sub-agents.

skills.name: implement
skills.description: Build the work described by a spec or ticket: draft feature-driven tests, implement to green, then add property-based stability tests — all on the minimalism ladder, run the over-engineering audit, close with code-review, then commit.

skills.name: bootstrap
skills.description: Bootstrap a repo for the strict development workflow: discover issue tracker, triage labels, and docs layout, then write docs/agents/*.

skills.name: to-spec
skills.description: Turn the current conversation into a spec (Problem, Solution, User Stories, Decisions), update docs/agents/, and publish it as a GitHub issue. Validates a feature before any code is written.

skills.name: to-tickets
skills.description: Break a spec, plan, or conversation into tracer-bullet tickets, each declaring its blocking edges, published as GitHub sub-issues.

skills.name: triage
skills.description: Move issues and external PRs through a state machine of triage roles: categorise, verify, grill if needed, and write agent-ready briefs.
